Piet Mondrian

13 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Amsterdam Skyline Viewed from the West
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · 1894–1904 · Watercolor
Landzicht Farm: Compositional Study
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · 1900–1910 · Fabricated charcoal on blue laid paper
Geinrust Farm: Close Frontal View
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · 1900–1910 · Pastel and charcoal on buff wove paper
Field with Young Trees in the Foreground
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · c. 1907 · oil on paper laid on board
Landscape at Loosduinen
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands, early 20th Century · 1905 · black chalk (extended with water in places), watercolor, and gouache; framing line in graphite (bottom edge)
Chrysanthemum
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · 1906–42 · oil on canvas
Farm near Duivendrecht
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · 1911–1921 · Oil on canvas
Lozenge Composition with Yellow, Black, Blue, Red, and Gray
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · Modernism · 1921 · Oil on canvas
Composition with Red, Yellow, and Blue
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · 1927 · oil on canvas
Composition with Red, Blue and Yellow
by Piet Mondrian
De Stijl · 1930
Black lines, three primary colors, white negative space, canvas flat as the grid itself. Mondrian believed he had found the underlying logic of visible reality.
Composition (No. 1) Gray-Red
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · 1935 · Oil on canvas
Study for a Composition
by Piet Mondrian
Netherlands · 1940–1941 · Collage of cut and pasted papers
Broadway Boogie Woogie
by Piet Mondrian
Piet Mondrian · 1943 · Museum of Modern Art
Mondrian's last completed painting, made after he fled wartime Europe for New York. The jittering yellow grid mimics Manhattan's street plan and the jazz he had fallen in love with; black bars are banished for the first time in decades.
←Salado branch of the Mogollon Claude Monet→
